hero.name: Steven Leal T.
hero.description: Student of Colombian software analyst and developer 🇨🇴. Specialized in high-performance web development: scalable architectures, clean code, and business-oriented delivery.

howIWork.title: My approach: Think before you code
howIWork.subtitle: Software development is an engineering solution, not a syntax exercise. In SpeakUp, the code was the final result of an architecture process, not the starting point.

architecture.title: Design before you implement
architecture.text: While the academic requirement asked for something simple, I chose to structure the project professionally. The docs/ folder in my repository is not decoration: I documented the system's protocols and identity before writing the first line.

validation.title: Data integrity from end to end
validation.text: Beyond basic HTML/JS validation, I implemented Zod + React Hook Form to ensure typed data throughout the entire flow. By keeping schemas completely independent from the UI, the system is modular and scalable.

solid.title: Modularity and SOLID Principles
solid.text: Instead of writing scattered code, I guided the UI development under the SOLID principles. Understanding these concepts led me to design pieces like Button, Container, and Badge with a single responsibility: they are not generic copies — they are purpose-built for the system, with consistent variants and strict typing.

growth.title: Going beyond the standard
growth.text: Although the requirement only demanded a basic command of HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and Git, I understood that a developer should never settle for the minimum. I chose to explore advanced technologies and design an architecture that truly reflected the pedagogical domain of SpeakUp.

projectCard.title: SpeakUp Institute
projectCard.alt: Screenshot of the SpeakUp Institute mobile interface, showing a simulated code editor with a React component and the message We are refactoring education.
projectCard.figcaption: A personal challenge designed to independently master React, TypeScript, and industry-grade tooling.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Open to job opportunities, internships, and collaborative projects where I can continue growing and contribute high-quality software solutions.
